rules.title: Fourth Amendment — Unreasonable Seizure
rules.source: U.S. Const. amend. IV; Terry v. Ohio, 392 U.S. 1 (1968)
rules.summary: A stop requires reasonable, articulable suspicion of criminal activity. Mere presence or refusal to answer is insufficient.

rules.title: Excessive Force
rules.summary: Force must be objectively reasonable under the circumstances. Factors: severity of crime, immediate threat, active resistance.

rules.title: Miranda Warning Required
rules.summary: Custodial interrogation requires warning of right to silence and counsel before questioning.

rules.title: Terry Frisk Scope
rules.summary: Pat-down limited to outer clothing for weapons when officer reasonably believes subject is armed and dangerous.

rules.title: First Amendment — Protected Speech
rules.source: U.S. Const. amend. I; City of Houston v. Hill, 482 U.S. 451 (1987)
rules.summary: Verbal criticism of police, recording officers, and refusal to answer are protected. Arrest on this basis alone is unlawful.

rules.title: De-escalation Duty
rules.source: DOJ Use-of-Force Policy (2022); IACP Model Policy
rules.summary: Officers should use verbal de-escalation, time, distance and cover when feasible before force.

rules.title: Arrest Without Probable Cause
rules.source: U.S. Const. amend. IV; Beck v. Ohio, 379 U.S. 89 (1964)
rules.summary: Arrest requires probable cause — facts sufficient for a prudent person to believe a crime was committed.

rules.title: Officer Identification
rules.source: State and municipal policy (most US jurisdictions)
rules.summary: Officer must identify themselves and state reason for the stop when asked.
